source.sourcePath: raw/sources/RA/01_DHF/02_ADD (HAD1417MCW, HAD1717MCW)/05_설계개발 검증 및 유효성 확인/11_Clinical Evaluation/Clinical Evaluation Plan (Doc. No. A-TCF-5-063).docx

document.title: Clinical Evaluation Plan for ADD Digital Flat Panel X-ray Detector
authorityOrJurisdiction: Council Directive 93/42/EEC as amended by Directive 2007/47/EC

sourceSummary.oneLine: Clinical evaluation plan for the ADD digital flat panel X-ray detector, prepared to support safety, performance, benefit-risk, equivalence, PMS and PMCF evaluation.
sourceSummary.officialUse: Intended for authorized competent authorities and notified bodies of H&abyz Co.,Ltd.
keyPoints: ADD is a wired or wireless digital flat panel X-ray detector for general radiographic diagnosis of human anatomy in adults and children, excluding mammography.
The device is classified as Class IIa by Rule 16 of Annex IX, MDD 93/42/EEC as amended by Directive 2007/47/EEC.
The plan states clinical evaluation will use literature review, equivalent predicate device data, and post-market surveillance data in accordance with MEDDEV 2.7.1 Rev.4.
The document identifies relevant essential requirements from MDD Annex I, including safety, performance, and side-effect acceptability.
The document states risk management is according to EN ISO 14971:2019.

concepts.name: Clinical evaluation
concepts.evidence: Clinical evaluation is regarded as an ongoing process conducted throughout the life cycle of a medical device.

concepts.name: Intended use
concepts.evidence: ADD(Abyz Digital Detector), Digital Flat Panel X-Ray Detector is indicated for digital imaging solution designed for providing general radiographic diagnosis of human anatomy targeting both adult and children.

concepts.name: Contraindications
concepts.evidence: Not to be used for mammography and direct cardiac application. Not to be used for infant and pregnant women.

concepts.name: Demonstration of equivalence
concepts.evidence: We are going to demonstrate equivalence according to the Demonstration of Equivalence requirements of MEDDEV 2.7/1 Rev.4

concepts.name: Post Market Clinical Follow-up
concepts.evidence: MEDDEV 2.12/2 Rev.2 Post Market Clinical Follow-up Studies

concepts.name: Post-market surveillance
concepts.evidence: This clinical evaluation includes the overall results of literature review, extensive clinical data for substantially equivalent predicate device and post market surveillance data in accordance with MEDDEV 2.7.1 Rev.4.

concepts.name: Risk management
concepts.evidence: this clinical evaluation is part on the risk management process according to EN ISO 14971:2019.

concepts.name: MDD Essential Requirements
concepts.evidence: The conformity to the following Essential Requirements (MDD 93/42/EEC Annex I) is assessed: Essential Requirement 1: Safety Requirements; Essential Requirement 3: Performance Requirements; Essential Requirement 6: Side-effect Requirements

evidence.claim: The document is Clinical Evaluation Plan report number A-TCF-5-063, Rev.3, for ADD Digital Flat Panel X-ray Detector.
evidence.evidenceText: Clinical Evaluation Plan for ADD Digital Flat Panel X-ray Detector Report No.: A-TCF-5-063 (Rev.3)

evidence.claim: The document was prepared, reviewed, and approved on Dec 01, 2022.
evidence.evidenceText: Prepared Quality team Han jimin Dec 01, 2022; Review R&D Manager Lee, Dong jin Dec 01, 2022; Approval Quality Manager Hur Namkyu Dec 01, 2022

evidence.claim: Revision 3 was issued for revision according to the update of applicable standards.
evidence.evidenceText: 4 3 Revision according to the update of applicable standards. 2022.12.01

evidence.claim: The plan states the device follows EN 60601-1 for safety, EN 60601-1-2 for electromagnetic compatibility, and EN 62220-1-1 for digital X-ray imaging device function.
evidence.evidenceText: For the safety of the device, the device is followed by (EN 60601-1), For electromagnetic compatibility, the device is followed by (EN 60601-1-2), For the digital X-ray imaging devices function followed by (EN 62220-1-1).

evidence.claim: The manufacturer is H&abyz Co.,Ltd. in Yongin-si, Gyeonggi-do, Republic of Korea.
evidence.evidenceText: Manufacturer H&abyz Co.,Ltd. Address 2-dong, 41-16, cheoinseong-ro, Namsa-eup, Cheoin-gu, Yongin-si, Gyeonggi-do, 17118, Republic of Korea

evidence.claim: ADD is a wired or wireless digital flat panel detector designed for digital radiography systems.
evidence.evidenceText: The ADD is wired or wireless digital flat panel detector that have been designed for faster, more streamlined approach to digital radiography systems.

evidence.claim: ADD uses TFT glass, CsI scintillators, amorphous silicon technology, photodiodes, and Thin Film Transistors.
evidence.evidenceText: The ADD detector utilize a combination of propriety TFT glass and scintillators (CsI)... The flat panel sensors of the ADD are fabricated using thin film technology based on amorphous silicon technology... Each pixel in the array consists of a light-sensing photodiode and a switching Thin Film Transistor (TFT)

evidence.claim: The device supports auto-trigger signal sensing technology for use without generator integration.
evidence.evidenceText: The detectors support an auto-trigger signal sensing technology that allows the detectors to be used without generator integration.

evidence.claim: The device is classified as Class IIa by Rule 16 of Annex IX under MDD 93/42/EEC as amended by Directive 2007/47/EEC.
evidence.evidenceText: Classification Class IIa by Rule 16 of Annex IX, MDD 93/42/EEC as amended by Directive 2007/47/EEC.

evidence.claim: The intended use is general radiographic diagnosis of human anatomy for adults and children, replacing film-based radiographic diagnostic systems, and not for mammography.
evidence.evidenceText: ADD (Abyz Digital Detector), Digital flat panel x-ray detector is indicated for digital imaging solution designed for providing general radiographic diagnosis of human anatomy targeting both adult and children. It is intended to replace film based radiographic diagnostic systems and provide more precise case diagnosis for physicians and radiologists. Not to be used for mammography.

evidence.claim: Contraindications include mammography, direct cardiac application, infant use, and pregnant women.
evidence.evidenceText: Not to be used for mammography and direct cardiac application. Not to be used for infant and pregnant women.

evidence.claim: The target patient population is adults and children except infants and pregnant women.
evidence.evidenceText: Target Patient Population A Adult and children except infant and pregnant women.

evidence.claim: The target user group includes radiologists and users who can read and understand the user manual.
evidence.evidenceText: Target User Group A a) Education: at least have the ability to read the manual b) Knowledge: User must be read and understand user manual... d) Experience: Radiologist

evidence.claim: The device is not disposable, not invasive, and not implantable.
evidence.evidenceText: Disposable Device N/A It is not disposable device; Invasive Device N/A It is not invasive device; Implantable Device N/A It is not implantable device

evidence.claim: The clinical evaluation includes literature review, substantially equivalent predicate device clinical data, and post-market surveillance data in accordance with MEDDEV 2.7.1 Rev.4.
evidence.evidenceText: This clinical evaluation includes the overall results of literature review, extensive clinical data for substantially equivalent predicate device and post market surveillance data in accordance with MEDDEV 2.7.1 Rev.4.

evidence.claim: The clinical evaluation is part of risk management according to EN ISO 14971:2019.
evidence.evidenceText: Therefore, this clinical evaluation is part on the risk management process according to EN ISO 14971:2019.

evidence.claim: The plan evaluates conformity to MDD Essential Requirements 1, 3, and 6.
evidence.evidenceText: The conformity to the following Essential Requirements (MDD 93/42/EEC Annex I) is assessed: Essential Requirement 1: Safety Requirements Essential Requirement 3: Performance Requirements Essential Requirement 6: Side-effect Requirements

evidence.claim: The plan states equivalence demonstration will address clinical, technical, and biological characteristics.
evidence.evidenceText: Equivalence demonstration will be asserted in terms of three characteristics (clinical, technical, and biological).

evidence.claim: Literature searches are planned using PubMed and ScienceDirect.
evidence.evidenceText: The “PUBMED” and “Science Direct” are going to utilize to search for literature and to identify any published scientific data

evidence.claim: The literature search date range is 2020.01.05 to 2020.06.15, and the issue date range of published literature is 2000 to 2019.
evidence.evidenceText: Date of Search: 2020.01.05 ~ 2020.06.15... Issue date of published literature: 2000 ~ 2019

evidence.claim: ADD has been tested and found to comply with limits for medical devices in IEC 60601-1.
evidence.evidenceText: ADD has been tested and found to comply with the limits for medical devices in IEC 60601-1.

evidence.claim: Sales approval status for South Korea, EU, U.S.A., and China is listed as TBD.
evidence.evidenceText: Sales Approval Status The following table shows the status of ADD licensing by country. Country License Status South Korea TBD EU TBD U.S.A. TBD China TBD

evidence.claim: The conclusion section states that risks associated with use of the device are acceptable when weighed against benefits to the patient.
evidence.evidenceText: The risks associated with the use of the device are acceptable when weighed against the benefits to the patient.

reviewFlags.reason: Sales approval statuses for South Korea, EU, U.S.A., and China are listed as TBD, so market authorization status is not established in the source.
Several clinical evaluation sections are written prospectively with placeholders or future-tense wording, including literature tables, appraisal results, and final signatures.
Authority signal is general; the document references EU MDD and RED requirements but does not identify a specific competent authority or notified body.
The source path and metadata mention HAD1417MCW and HAD1717MCW/productSignal HAD/A/F 1417-1717, but the document text identifies the model name as ADD.
Some tables contain blank fields or TBD values for sales, complaints, vigilance data, literature entries, and next clinical evaluation date.
